preferred_assay_titles.10x multiome: A single-cell assay for simultaneously profiling gene expression (scRNA-seq) and chromatin accessibility (scATAC-seq) from the same sample.
preferred_assay_titles.10x multiome with MULTI-seq: Combines 10x Multiome with MULTI-seq, enabling simultaneous profiling of gene expression, chromatin accessibility, and sample multiplexing.
preferred_assay_titles.10x snATAC-seq with Scale pre-indexing: 10X snATAC-seq with Scale pre-indexing is a ATAC-seq method that uses combinatorial barcoding (Tn5-based pre-indexing followed by 10X cell indexing) to label the cellular origin of open chromatin regions. The combinatorial barcoding allows "superloading" of 10X lanes increasing the throughput to hundreds of thousands of cells per reaction.
preferred_assay_titles.AAV-MPRA: Adeno-Associated Virus Massively Parallel Reporter Assay (AAV-MPRA) is used to evaluate the activity of regulatory elements by introducing them into cells via AAV vectors.
preferred_assay_titles.ACCESS-ATAC: Accessible Chromatin by Cytosine Editing Site Sequencing with ATAC-seq (ACCESS-ATAC) is used to map chromatin accessibility and transcription factor binding at high resolution by integrating cytosine deamination with ATAC-seq.
preferred_assay_titles.Arrayed mN2H: Mammalian NanoLuc two-hybrid is a mammalian-based protein-protein interaction assay that uses reconstitution of split Nano-Luciferase to detect an interaction between pairs of proteins tested individually.
preferred_assay_titles.Arrayed semi-qY2H v1: Arrayed Semi-q Yeast two-hybrid (Y2H) v1 is a yeast-based protein-protein interaction assay that fuses proteins to DNA-binding and Activation domains and measures His3 and Ade2 reporter expression in the presences of increasing competitive inhibitors to detect an interaction between pairs of proteins tested individually using a low level of expression of a N-terminal AD fusion.
preferred_assay_titles.Arrayed semi-qY2H v2: Arrayed Semi-q Yeast two-hybrid (Y2H) v2 is a yeast-based protein-protein interaction assay that fuses proteins to DNA-binding and Activation domains a and measures His3 and Ade2 reporter expression in the presences of increasing competitive inhibitors to detect an interaction between pairs of proteins tested individually using a high level of expression of a N-terminal AD fusion.
preferred_assay_titles.Arrayed semi-qY2H v3: Arrayed Semi-q Yeast two-hybrid (Y2H) v3 is a yeast-based protein-protein interaction assay that fuses proteins to DNA-binding and Activation domains and measures His3 and Ade2 reporter expression in the presences of increasing competitive inhibitors to detect an interaction between pairs of proteins tested individually using a high level of expression of a C-terminal AD fusion.
preferred_assay_titles.Arrayed yN2H: Yeast NanoLuc two-hybrid is a yeast-based protein-protein interaction assay that uses reconstitution of split Nano-Luciferase to detect an interaction between pairs of proteins tested individually.
preferred_assay_titles.ATAC-seq: Assay for Transposase-Accessible Chromatin using sequencing (ATAC-seq) measures chromatin accessibility by identifying open chromatin regions.
preferred_assay_titles.Bisulfite-seq: Bisulfite sequencing (Bisulfite-seq) is used to detect DNA methylation by treating DNA with sodium bisulfite, which converts unmethylated cytosines to uracil, allowing the identification of methylated cytosines at single-base resolution through sequencing.
preferred_assay_titles.CC-Perturb-seq: A type of Perturb-seq that combines a plate-based split-pool combinatorial barcode indexing with circularization-based capture of Pol III gRNA transcripts.
preferred_assay_titles.Cell painting: A morphological profiling assay that multiplexes fluorescent dyes to reveal relevant cellular components or organelles.
preferred_assay_titles.CRISPR FACS screen: A CRISPR-based screen that employs Fluorescence-Activated Cell Sorting (FACS) to separate and analyze cell populations based on fluorescence markers.
preferred_assay_titles.CRISPR FlowFISH screen: A CRISPR-based screen that uses Flow Cytometry and Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization (FlowFISH) to measure the impact of genetic perturbations on RNA expression levels.
preferred_assay_titles.CRISPR MACS screen: A CRISPR-based screen that employs Magnetic-Activated Cell Sorting CRISPR screen (MACS) which uses magnetic beads conjugated to specific antibodies or markers to isolate and analyze cells based on surface protein expression.
preferred_assay_titles.CRISPR mCherry screen: A CRISPR-based screen that uses mCherry fluorescent protein as a reporter to assess gene knockout, activation, or repression effects on cellular processes.
preferred_assay_titles.CRISPR tiling screen allelic readout: A CRISPR tiling screen using direct targeted sequencing of edited endogenous alleles and fluorescence-activated cell sorting to achieve nucleotide-resolution genotype-phenotype mapping.
preferred_assay_titles.CRISPR tiling screen guide readout: A CRISPR tiling screen using guide RNA count-based readout and fluorescence-activated cell sorting to map functional genomic regions.
preferred_assay_titles.CRISPR tiling screen reporter readout: A CRISPR tiling screen using targeted sequencing of an exogenous reporter sequence to measure guide-specific editing outcomes.
preferred_assay_titles.CROP-seq: A single-cell CRISPR screen that captures guide RNAs as part of the polyadenylated transcriptome, enabling direct linkage of perturbation and transcriptional response without separate barcoding.
preferred_assay_titles.CUT&RUN: Cleavage Under Targets and Release Using Nuclease (CUT&RUN) maps protein-DNA interactions, such as transcription factor binding or histone modifications, by targeting specific proteins with antibodies and releasing nearby DNA fragments using MNase.
preferred_assay_titles.DNase-seq: DNase I hypersensitive sites sequencing is for mapping DNase I hypersensitive sites across the genome to identify regulatory regions like promoters and enhancers.
preferred_assay_titles.DUAL-IPA: DUAL-Impact on Protein Abundance, a fluorescence-based assay designed to assess the effects of missense variants on protein abundance. It compares the fluorescence of GFP-tagged proteins in HEK293T cells expressing either the reference allele or variant allele.
preferred_assay_titles.electroporated MPRA: Electroporated MPRA (Massively Parallel Reporter Assay) is used to evaluate the activity of regulatory elements by introducing them into cells via electroporation.
preferred_assay_titles.Hi-C: High-throughput Chromosome Conformation Capture (Hi-C) is used to study the three-dimensional structure of genomes by capturing and analyzing interactions between different regions of DNA.
preferred_assay_titles.HiCAR: Hi-C on Accessible Regulatory DNA (HiCAR) integrates Tn5 transposase and chromatin proximity ligation to map long-range interactions of open chromatin regions.
preferred_assay_titles.Histone ChIP-seq: Chromatin immunoprecipitation sequencing (ChIP-seq) maps histone modifications to study their genome-wide distribution.
preferred_assay_titles.Immune-SGE: A cloning-free CRISPR-based assay performed in primary immune cells that systematically introduces and evaluates the functional impact of hundreds to thousands of nucleotide substitutions, insertions, or deletions at a specific genomic locus.
preferred_assay_titles.in vivo MPRA: In vivo Massively Parallel Reporter Assay (MPRA) evaluates the functional activity of regulatory elements by measuring reporter gene expression in a living organism.
preferred_assay_titles.in vivo Perturb-seq: In vivo Perturb-seq combines CRISPR-based perturbation with single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) in a living organism.
preferred_assay_titles.LABEL-seq: Labeling with barcodes and enrichment for biochemical analysis (LABEL-seq) is an assay that leverages massively parallel sequencing to measure the effect of thousands of missense variants on protein abundance, activity or protein-protein interactions.
preferred_assay_titles.lentiMPRA: Lentiviral Massively Parallel Reporter Assay (lentiMPRA) is used to evaluate the activity of regulatory elements by introducing them into cells via lentiviral vectors.
preferred_assay_titles.MAVE: Multiplex assays of variant effect (MAVE) measure the functional consequences of all possible variants in disease-relevant loci for a variety of molecular and cellular phenotypes.
preferred_assay_titles.miDOGMA-seq: An adaptation of CITE-seq (Cellular Indexing of Transcriptomes and Epitopes by sequencing) for measuring gene activity across the central dogma of gene regulation.
preferred_assay_titles.Migration CRISPR screen: A CRISPR-based screen used to identify genes and regulatory elements that influence cell motility.
preferred_assay_titles.MPRA: Massively Parallel Reporter Assay (MPRA) evaluates the functional activity of regulatory elements by measuring reporter gene expression.
preferred_assay_titles.MPRA (scQer): MPRA single-cell Quantitative Expression Reporter (scQer) is a single-cell MPRA variant that uses dual RNA cassettes to separate detection and quantification of reporter gene expression.
preferred_assay_titles.mtscMultiome: mitochondrial single-cell Multiome (mtscMultiome) combines 10x multiome with single-cell mitochondrial sequencing (scMito-seq) to profile mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA), chromatin accessibility, variants and transcriptional activity in individual cells.
preferred_assay_titles.Multiome Perturb-seq: An extension of Perturb-seq that simultaneously profiles perturbation-induced changes in gene expression and chromatin accessibility in single cells, enabling integrated modeling of regulatory effects on the transcriptome and epigenome.
preferred_assay_titles.ONT direct WGS: An assay to determine the entire sequence of an organism's genome. This assay specifically utilizes Oxford Nanopore sequencing platforms.
preferred_assay_titles.ONT dRNA: An assay measuring gene expression by directly sequencing RNA instead of converting RNA to cDNA. This assay specifically utilizes Oxford Nanopore sequencing platforms.
preferred_assay_titles.ONT Fiber-seq: A DNA methylation profiling assay in which chromatin stencils are created by treating nuclei with m6A methyltransferase (MTase) and then sequenced using single-molecule circular consensus sequencing to identify regions of accessible DNA at nucleotide resolution.
preferred_assay_titles.Parse Perturb-seq: A type of Perturb-seq that uses a plate based split-pool combinatorial barcode indexing to scale up a scRNA-seq
preferred_assay_titles.Parse SPLiT-seq: Parse Split Pool Ligation-based Transcriptome sequencing (Parse SPLiT-seq) is a single-cell RNA-seq method that uses combinatorial barcoding to label the cellular origin of RNA.
preferred_assay_titles.Perturb-seq: A single-cell CRISPR screen that combines CRISPR-based gene perturbation with single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq).
preferred_assay_titles.perturb-SHARE-seq: perturb-SHARE-seq is an extension of SHARE-seq that captures single cell perturbation barcodes alongside gene expression and chromatin accessibility profiles.
preferred_assay_titles.Pooled Y2H: Pooled Yeast two-hybrid (Y2H) is a yeast-based protein-protein interaction assay that fuses proteins to DNA-binding and Activation domains and measures His3 reporter expression to detect an interaction between pairs of proteins tested as a pool using a low level of expression of a N-terminal AD fusion.
preferred_assay_titles.Proliferation CRISPR screen: A CRISPR-based screen that measures cell survival or proliferation as the primary readout.
preferred_assay_titles.RNA-seq: RNA sequencing (RNA-seq) is used to analyze the transcriptome by sequencing RNA.
preferred_assay_titles.scACCESS-ATAC: Single-cell Accessible Chromatin by Cytosine Editing Site Sequencing with ATAC-seq (scACCESS-ATAC) is used to profile chromatin accessibility and transcription factor binding at high resolution in single cells.
preferred_assay_titles.scATAC-seq: Single-cell ATAC-seq (scATAC-seq) profiles chromatin accessibility at single-cell resolution.
preferred_assay_titles.scCRISPR screen: A CRISPR-based screen that combines CRISPR perturbations with single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) to analyze gene expression changes and cellular phenotypes at the single-cell level.
preferred_assay_titles.scMultiome-NT-seq: Single-cell multiomic sequencing of chromatin state and metabolically labeled new RNA (scMultiome-NT-seq) enables joint analysis of chromatin accessibility, newly transcribed and pre-existing mRNAs in single cells.
preferred_assay_titles.scNT-seq: Single-cell metabolically labeled new RNA tagging sequencing (scNT-seq) enables parallel analysis of newly transcribed and pre-existing mRNAs in single cells.
preferred_assay_titles.scNT-seq2: An improved version of scNT-seq that employs optimized second-strand synthesis to enhance mapping rate and library complexity.
preferred_assay_titles.scNT-seq3: An improved version of scNT-seq that employs in situ chemical conversion to enhance throughput and scalability using the 10x Genomics droplet microfluidics platform.
preferred_assay_titles.scRNA-seq: Single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) profiles gene expression by sequencing RNA from individual cells.
preferred_assay_titles.SGE: A CRISPR-based assay that systematically introduces and evaluates the functional impact of hundreds to thousands of nucleotide substitutions, insertions, or deletions at a specific genomic locus.
preferred_assay_titles.SGE-RNA: A CRISPR-based assay that systematically introduces and evaluates the functional impact of hundreds to thousands of nucleotide substitutions, insertions, or deletions at a specific genomic locus, with read out from RNA collected to distinguish variants that may disrupt protein function from those that affect RNA expression or splicing.
preferred_assay_titles.SHARE-seq: Simultaneous high-throughput ATAC and RNA expression with sequencing (SHARE-seq) is used for the measurement of chromatin accessibility and gene expression in the same single cell.
preferred_assay_titles.snATAC-seq: Single-nucleus ATAC-seq (snATAC-seq) profiles chromatin accessibility in individual nuclei.
preferred_assay_titles.snM3C-seq: Single-nucleus methyl-3C sequencing (snM3C-seq) captures chromatin organization and DNA methylation information and robustly separates heterogeneous cell types.
preferred_assay_titles.snMCT-seq: Single-nucleus methylcytosine and transcriptome sequencing (snmCT-seq), a multi-omic method that requires no physical separation of DNA and RNA molecules.
preferred_assay_titles.snRNA-seq: Single-nucleus RNA sequencing (snRNA-seq) profiles gene expression by sequencing RNA from isolated nuclei.
preferred_assay_titles.snRNA-seq with Scale pre-indexing: snRNA-seq with Scale pre-indexing is a RNA-seq method that uses combinatorial barcoding (Tn5-based pre-indexing) to label the cellular origin of open chromatin regions. The combinatorial barcoding allows "superloading" of sequencing lanes increasing the throughput to hundreds of thousands of cells per reaction.
preferred_assay_titles.Spatial transcriptomics: A molecular profiling technique that maps gene expression patterns within intact tissue sections.
preferred_assay_titles.STARR-seq: STARR-seq (Self-Transcribing Active Regulatory Region sequencing) is used to identify and quantify enhancer activity by enabling direct transcription of candidate regulatory regions into reporter RNA.
preferred_assay_titles.TAP-seq: A high-throughput, cost-efficient variant of Perturb-seq that focuses single-cell RNA-seq coverage on specific genes of interest.
preferred_assay_titles.TF ChIP-seq: Transcription Factor ChIP-seq (TF ChIP-seq) maps genome-wide transcription factor binding sites.
preferred_assay_titles.VAMP-seq: Variant Abundance by Massively Parallel Sequencing (VAMP-seq) uses massively parallel sequencing to measure the effects of thousands of missense variants on intracellular protein abundance.
preferred_assay_titles.VAMP-seq (MultiSTEP): Variant of VAMP-seq that measures the effects of missense variants on secretion and post-translational modification of secreted proteins using a cell surface display method.
preferred_assay_titles.varACCESS: A high-throughput assay that integrates genome variants into chromatin accessibility analysis.
preferred_assay_titles.Variant painting via fluorescence: A modification of Cell Painting that employs fluorescent protein fusions to protein coding variants to characterize localization alongside cellular morphology.
preferred_assay_titles.Variant painting via immunostaining: A modification of Cell Painting that employs epitope tagging and immunostaining of protein coding variants to characterize localization alongside cellular morphology.
preferred_assay_titles.Variant-EFFECTS: A CRISPR-based screen that combines CRISPR prime editing, RNA fluorescence in situ hybridization (FlowFISH), and cell sorting to quantitatively measure the effects of designed DNA sequence variants on gene expression.
preferred_assay_titles.WGS: Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS) is an assay to determine the entire sequence of an organism's genome.

software.8cube-manuscript-subsample: A script for subsampling anndata files.
software.abc-enhancer-gene-prediction: Cell type specific enhancer-gene predictions using ABC model (Fulco, Nasser et al, Nature Genetics 2019)
software.ADASTRA: Database of transcription factor allele-specific binding from ChIP-seq data
software.aloft: ALoFT provides extensive annotations to putative loss-of-function variants (LoF) in protein-coding genes including functional, evolutionary and network features.
software.alphamissense: AlphaMissense is an adaptation of AlphaFold fine-tuned on human and primate variant population frequency databases to predict missense variant pathogenicity.
software.anndata: Anndata is a Python package for handling annotated data matrices.
software.archr: Robust and scaleable analysis of single-cell chromatin accessibility data
software.bamtools: C++ API & command-line toolkit for working with BAM data.
software.bcalm: BCalm is a package that provides a modification from the mpralm package, an R package that provides tools for differential analysis in MPRA studies. BCalm allows users to use individual barcodes as model input.
software.bedgraphtobigwig: Convert a bedGraph file to bigWig format.
software.bedtobigbed: bedToBigBed takes a standard bed file or a non-standard bed file with associated .as file to create a compressed bigBed version.  Description of Big Binary Indexed (BBI) files and visualization of next-generation sequencing experiment results explained by W.J. Kent, PMCID: PMC2922891.
software.bedtools: Collectively, the bedtools utilities are a swiss-army knife of tools for a wide-range of genomics analysis tasks. The most widely-used tools enable genome arithmetics: that is, set theory on the genome. For example, bedtools allows one to intersect, merge, count, complement, and shuffle genomic intervals from multiple files in widely-used genomic file formats such as BAM, BED, GFF/GTF, and VCF.
software.bird: BIRD (Bayesian Inference of Regulatory Differences) is a software suite for identifying regulatory variants in data from STARR-seq and other massively parallel reporter assays (MPRAs).
software.bluestarr: Convolutional neural network for predicting STARR-seq effects.
software.BlueSTARR: Convolutional neural network for predicting STARR-seq effects.
software.bowtie: Bowtie is an ultrafast, memory-efficient short read aligner. It aligns short DNA sequences (reads) to the human genome at a rate of over 25 million 35-bp reads per hour. Bowtie indexes the genome with a Burrows-Wheeler index to keep its memory footprint small: typically about 2.2 GB for the human genome (2.9 GB for paired-end).
software.bowtie2: Bowtie 2 is an ultrafast and memory-efficient tool for aligning sequencing reads to long reference sequences.
software.bustools: Bustools is a program for manipulating BUS files for single cell RNA-Seq datasets.
software.bwa: BWA is a software package for mapping low-divergent sequences against a large reference genome, such as the human genome.
software.c2sg: Combining SNP-to-gene linking strategies to pinpoint disease genes and assess disease omnigenicity
software.cadd: Genome-wide single nucleotide variants and short indels effects/prioritization.
software.candidate Cis-Regulatory Elements: Candidate regulatory elements derived from DNase-seq experiments and labeled with Histone ChiP-seq expeiments
software.caQTL: Chromatin accessibility QTL (caQTL) analysis identifies genetic variants that influence chromatin accessibility at regulatory elements.
software.cellranger: Cell Ranger is a set of analysis pipelines that process Chromium single cell data to align reads, generate feature-barcode matrices, perform clustering and other secondary analysis, and more.
software.cellranger-arc: Cell Ranger ARC offers a suite of analysis pipelines for processing Chromium Single Cell Multiome ATAC + Gene Expression sequencing data
software.cellranger-atac: This is the main Cell Ranger ATAC pipeline that performs alignment, de-duplication, filtering, generates feature-barcode matrices, and secondary analysis
software.cellsnp-lite: Cellsnp-lite is a C/C++ tool for efficient genotyping bi-allelic SNPs on single cells.
software.clinpred: ClinPred is an efficient tool for identifying disease-relevant nonsynonymous variants.
software.colocboost: ColocBoost is an R package for colocalization analysis across multiple genetic association studies using a multi-task variable selection regression framework. It handles correlated predictors and sparse effects, providing frequentist statistical evidence for shared genetic signals across traits.
software.content: Fast and powerful TWAS in single-cell RNA-Seq experiments
software.cooler: Cooler is a Python support library for .cool/.mcool files: an efficient storage format for high resolution genomic interaction matrices.
software.countess: CountESS analysis tool for deep mutational scanning
software.COXPRESdb: Database of co-expression relationships between genes
software.CRISPR screen: CRISPR screen is a technique used to identify genes that are essential for a particular biological process or phenotype.
software.crispr-bean: bean improves CRISPR pooled screen analysis by 1) unconfounding variable per-guide editing outcome by considering genotypic outcome from reporter sequence and 2) through accurate modeling of screen procedure.
software.crispr-correct: CRISPR-Correct was developed by the Pinello Lab as an easy-to-use Python package for performing guide RNA mapping given the raw FASTQs and guide library dataframe.
software.crispr-millipede: CRISPR-Millipede was developed by the Pinello Lab as an easy-to-use Python package for processing targeted amplicon-sequencing of tiled sequences from base-editing tiling screens to identify functional nucleotides.
software.crispr-surf: Design and analyses of tiling screens
software.crispresso2: Quantification of genome editing from targeted or WGS sequencing data
software.crispri-tiling-design: Design gRNA pools for CRISPRi tiling screens including selecting elements/promoters, scoring and selecting gRNAs, and adding control gRNAs
software.crispriscreen: Map enhancer-gene connections from TAP-seq/Perturb-seq experiments differential expression testing
software.crisprme: Enumeration of potential off-target sites taking into account genetic variance
software.cutadapt: Cutadapt finds and removes adapter sequences, primers, poly-A tails and other types of unwanted sequence from your high-throughput sequencing reads.
software.cv2f: A consensus variant-to-function (cV2F) score to functionally prioritize variants for disease.
software.cV2F: A consensus variant-to-function (cV2F) score to functionally prioritize variants for disease.
software.dann: A deep neural network (DNN) algorithm for deleterious annotation of genetic variants using neural networks (DANN).
software.deMULTIplex2: deMULTIplex2 is a mechanism-guided classification algorithm for multiplexed scRNA-seq data that successfully recovers many more cells across a spectrum of challenging datasets compared to existing methods.
software.demuxafy: Demultiplexing and detecting doublets is an important part of droplet-based scRNA-seq processing pipelines.
software.deogen2: DEOGEN2 is a predictor of missense SVNs for human proteins.
software.deseq2: Estimate variance-mean dependence in count data from high-throughput sequencing assays and test for differential expression based on a model using the negative binomial distribution.
software.distal-regulation-crispr-data: Re-analyze and harmonize Gasperini et al 2019,  Schraivogel et al 2020, and Fulco et al2019 data for ENCODE distal regulation E-G benchmarking
software.dna_r10.4.1_e8.2_400bps_sup@v5.2.0: A basecalling model that can be utilized with the Oxford Nanopore Dorado software.
software.dna_r10.4.1_e8.2_400bps_sup@v5.2.0_5mCG_: A basecalling model that can be utilized with the Oxford Nanopore Dorado software.
software.dna_r10.4.1_e8.2_400bps_sup@v5.2.0_6mA@v: A basecalling model that can be utilized with the Oxford Nanopore Dorado software.
software.dnn_treepgm: Hamiltonian Monte Carlo sampling on neural networks based on infinite tree-structured probabilistic graphical models for uncertainty quantification on predictive models.
software.dogme: A nextflow pipeline for basecalling nanopore reads with and without modifications before mapping and processing.
software.dorado: Dorado is a high-performance, easy-to-use, open source basecaller for Oxford Nanopore reads.
software.drem: Gene Regulatory Networks trajectory analysis tool
software.dropseq_tools: Package for the analysis of Drop-seq data developed by Jim Nemesh in the McCarroll Lab.
software.dynast: Dynast is a command-line pipeline that preprocesses data from metabolic labeling scRNA-seq experiments and quantifies the following four mRNA species: unlabeled unspliced, unlabeled spliced, labeled unspliced and labeled spliced.
software.eigen_and_eigen_pc: Eigen is a spectral approach to the functional annotation of genetic variants in coding and noncoding regions.
software.ENCODE-rE2G: ENCODE regulatory element to gene (rE2G) analysis
software.engreitz-variant-effects-scripts: Scripts for generating variant effects on expression associated with Engreitz's Variant-EFFECTS data.
software.epiraction: The EPIraction algorithm uses Tikhonov-regularized least squares models to predict the interacting promoter-enhancer pairs.
software.eQTL: expression QTL (eQTL) analysis identifies genetic variants that influence gene expression.
software.eSIG-Net: Accurate prediction of single-mutation induced perturbations on protein interactions using a language model
software.ESM-1v: A protein language model used to predict all ~450 million possible missense variant effects in the human genome.
software.esm-1v-workflow: Workflow and supporting code for evaluating every amino acid human protein substitution using ESM-1v.
software.esm1b: A protein language model used to predict all ~450 million possible missense variant effects in the human genome.
software.eugene: EUGENe is a Python toolkit for building and evaluating sequence-based deep learning models in genomics.
software.eve: EVE is a model for the prediction of clinical significance of human variants based on sequences of diverse organisms across evolution.
software.excalibr: Multi-sample, multi-component skew normal mixture model calibrations of functional assay scores to ACMG/AMP guidelines
software.fastgxc: Fast and powerful cell-type specific xQTL mapping from single-cell RNA-Seq and ATAC-Seq data
software.fastq-process: The following program will process the fastq handle QC vs full & modify fastq header & split fastqs & add index & trim & split project
software.fastqc: A quality control tool for high throughput sequence data.
software.fathmm: A high-throughput web-server capable of predicting the functional consequences of both coding variants, i.e. non-synonymous single nucleotide variants (nsSNVs), and non-coding variants in the human genome.
software.fathmm-mkl: A high-throughput web-server capable of predicting the functional consequences of both coding variants, i.e. non-synonymous single nucleotide variants (nsSNVs), and non-coding variants in the human genome. The MKL algorithm integrates functional annotations from ENCODE with nucleotide-based HMMs.
software.fathmm-xf: A high-throughput web-server capable of predicting the functional consequences of both coding variants, i.e. non-synonymous single nucleotide variants (nsSNVs), and non-coding variants in the human genome. The XF algorithm incorporates extra features.
software.fba: fba is a flexible and streamlined toolbox for quality control, quantification, demultiplexing of various single-cell feature barcoding assays.
software.featureCounts: featureCounts is a highly efficient general-purpose read summarization program that counts mapped reads for genomic features such as genes, exons, promoter, gene bodies, genomic bins and chromosomal locations. It can be used to count both RNA-seq and genomic DNA-seq reads.
software.fitcons: fitCons, the fitness consequences of functional annotation, integrates functional assays (such as ChIP-Seq) with selective pressure inferred using the INSIGHT method.
software.fractel: FRACTEL is a software designed to find element-level significant discoveries applying a Robust Rank Aggregation (RRA) method to combine individual p-values.
software.gcsc: GCSC regression performs gene set enrichment analysis using gene expression data through the TWAS framework.
software.gmvp: gMVP is a graph attention neural network model designed to effectively represent or learn the representation of protein sequence and structure context to improve missense variant prediction of disease impact.
software.gnu-parallel: GNU parallel is a shell tool for executing jobs in parallel using one or more computers.
software.graphreg: GraphReg (Chromatin interaction aware gene regulatory modeling with graph attention networks) is a graph neural network based gene regulation model which integrates DNA sequence, 1D epigenomic data (such as chromatin accessibility and histone modifications), and 3D chromatin conformation data (such as Hi-C, HiChIP, Micro-C, HiCAR) to predict gene expression in an informative way.
software.gssg: GSSG is used for generating gene programs for analysis, linking SNPs to genes in a gene program to generate a genome-wide annotation, performing disease heritability enrichment analysis using S-LDSC
software.GVATdb: Database of transcription factor allele-specific binding derived from SELEX experiments
software.GWAS: Genome-wide association study (GWAS) is a genetic study that examines the relationship between genetic variants and a particular trait or disease.
software.harmonypy: Harmonypy is a port of the harmony R package for integrating multiple high-dimensional datasets.
software.hic-straw: Straw is library which allows rapid streaming of contact data from .hic files. This repository contains source code for the C++, R, Python, and MATLAB versions of Straw.
software.hicar-quantification: Script for generating bin paired count asscoiated with Gersbach lab's HiCAR data.
software.igvf-chromap: The igvf-chromap tool provides a command-line interface (CLI) with subcommands for managing the index creation and alignment steps.
software.igvf-cvfg-biobank: Python package for performing biobank validation of variant classifications in the All of Us Workbench.
software.igvf-kallisto-bustools: The igvf-kallisto-bustools tool provides a command-line interface (CLI) with several subcommands for managing the index creation and quantification steps.
software.igvf_camp_anno_prediction: Code used to reproduce the functional prediction scheme used as part of the IGVF CAMP WG Annotation Core Infastructure Project and the Phenotypic Impact FG Pillar Project.
software.kallisto: Kallisto is a program for quantifying abundances of transcripts from RNA-Seq data
software.kb-python: Kb-python is a python package for processing single-cell RNA-sequencing that wraps the Kallisto and Bustools tools.
software.kim_atac: Kim_ATAC leverages single-cell ATAC-seq to identify disease-critical fetal and adult brain cell types.
software.ldspec: LDSPEC (LD SNP-pair effect correlation regression) is a method for estimating the correlation of causal disease effect sizes for pairs of nearby SNPs, depending on their functional annotations.
software.list-s2: LIST-S2 (species specific) predicts the deleteriousness of amino acid mutations in protein sequences.
software.lsrrsrlfkotwmwmp-2024: Repository of code associated with "Long-read sequencing transcriptome quantification with lr-kallisto" by Rebekah Loving et al., 2024
software.m-cap: M-CAP is the first pathogenicity classifier for rare missense variants in the human genome that is tuned to the high sensitivity required in the clinic.
software.macs: Model-based Analysis of ChIP-Seq (MACS) is a software for identifying transcript factor binding sites.
software.mageck: Model-based Analysis of Genome-wide CRISPR-Cas9 Knockout (MAGeCK) is a computational tool to identify important genes from the recent genome-scale CRISPR-Cas9 knockout screens technology.
software.matplotlib: Matplotlib is a comprehensive library for creating static, animated, and interactive visualizations in Python. Matplotlib makes easy things easy and hard things possible.
software.mavevis: A tool to visualize variant effect maps from MaveDB https://www.mavedb.org/ as genophenograms with added tracks for structure information. MaveVis is deployed as a webtool at http://vis.varianteffect.org.
software.metaLR: Ensemble-based approach (logistic regression) that integrates multiple scoring methods.
software.metarnn: MetaRNN and MetaRNN-indel are pathogenicity prediction scores for human nonsynonymous SNVs (nsSNVs) and non-frameshift (NF) indels.
software.metasvm: Ensemble-based approaches (support vector machine) that integrates multiple scoring methods.
software.mgatk: the mgatk package, a python-based command line interface for processing .bam files with mitochondrial reads and generating high-quality heteroplasmy estimation from sequencing data. This package places a special emphasis on mitochondrial genotypes generated from single-cell genomics data, primarily mtscATAC-seq, but is generally applicable across other assays.
software.minimap2: Minimap2 aligns long reads to a reference genome.

software.modkit: A bioinformatics tool for working with modified bases from Oxford Nanopore.
software.mpc: MPC (Missense badness, PolyPhen-2, and Constraint) is a score (missense badness) that measures the increased deleteriousness of amino acid substitutions when they occur in missense-constrained regions.
software.mpraflow: MPRAflow scripts for generating the report experiment consisting of normalized counts for MPRA data.
software.mpralm: An R package that provides tools for differential analysis in MPRA studies.
software.mpranalyze: MPRAnalyze provides statistical framework for the analysis of data generated by Massively Parallel Reporter Assays (MPRAs), used to directly measure enhancer activity. MPRAnalyze can be used for quantification of enhancer activity, classification of active enhancers and comparative analyses of enhancer activity between conditions. MPRAnalyze construct a nested pair of generalized linear models (GLMs) to relate the DNA and RNA observations, easily adjustable to various experimental designs and conditions, and provides a set of rigorous statistical testig schemes.

software.mprasnakeflow-quantification: MPRAsnakeflow scripts for generating the report experiment consisting of normalized counts for MPRA data.
software.mutationassessor: A software to predict the functional impact of amino-acid substitutions in proteins, such as mutations discovered in cancer or missense polymorphisms.
software.mutationtaster_2: A software to evaluate the pathogenic potential of DNA sequence alterations.
software.mutpred2: Pathogenicity prediction for missense variants from MutPred2
software.MutPred2: Pathogenicity prediction for missense variants from MutPred2
software.mvp: MVP (Missense Variant Pathogenicity prediction) is a new prediction method that uses deep residual network to leverage large training data sets and many correlated predictors.
software.nanopore-bc-mapping-viral-mapping: Software for generating a barcode to element mapping table from Nanopore data.
software.numpy: NumPy is a software enabling numerical computing with Python.
software.pairsqc: Pairsqc is a tool for generating a QC report for a Hi-C pairs file.
software.pairtools: pairtools is a simple and fast command-line framework to process sequencing data from a Hi-C experiment.
software.pandas: Pandas is a data analysis and manipulation tool in Python.
software.parse-biosciences: Parse Biosciences provides a computational pipeline designed to process data generated from their SPLiT-seq–based single cell RNA sequencing assays. The software takes raw sequencing reads as input and produces outputs such as cell- and feature-level count matrices, sample demultiplexing, quality control metrics, and associated metadata. It is optimized for large-scale single cell experiments and integrates with downstream analysis tools by exporting standard formats like count matrices and annotations.
software.pear: PEAR assembles Illumina paired-end reads if the DNA fragment sizes are smaller than twice the length of reads. PEAR can assemble 95% of reads with 35-bp mean overlap with a false-positive rate of 0.004. PEAR also works with multiplexed data sets  where  the  true  underlying  DNA  fragment size varies. PEAR has an extremely low false-positive rate of 0.0003 on data sets where no overlap exists between the two reads (i.e. when DNA fragment sizes are larger than twice the read length).
software.personal-genomes: Reddy lab's Personal Genomes scripts for creating personal genomes for alignment of samples with known genetic variation, converts coordiantes of files aligned to the personal genome back to GRCh38, and counts alleles of the known variants.
software.perturbplan: PerturbPlan is an R package for power analysis in perturb-seq and TAP-seq experiments.
software.polyphen-2: PolyPhen-2 (Polymorphism Phenotyping v2) is a tool which predicts possible impact of an amino acid substitution on the structure and function of a human protein using straightforward physical and comparative considerations.
software.polypred: PolyPred, that improves polygenic prediction in diverse populations by leveraging functionally informed fine-mapping.
software.pooled_y2H-data_processing_script: Data processing script for Pooled Y2H.
software.pooled_y2H-well_binning_script: Well binning script for Pooled Y2H.
software.pQTL: Protein-quantitative trait loci (pQTL) analysis identifies genetic variants that influence protein expression.
software.primateai: PrimateAI is a deep residual neural network for classifying the pathogenicity of missense mutations.
software.prime:
PRIME (Probabilistic Regional Intolerance to Missense Estimation) is a Bayesian hierarchical modeling approach for measuring intolerance, or constraint, at sub-genic resolution across coding regions of the genome. The model estimates the probability that a variant observed in the population is missense within a genomic region using gnomAD v4 exome and genome sequencing data. Regions under stronger purifying selection are expected to have lower probabilities, reflecting a depletion of functional variants in the population.

Through its beta–binomial hierarchical structure, PRIME induces shrinkage on sub-genic estimates of intolerance by allowing them to be jointly informed by other regions within the same gene and by genes across the genome. This borrowing of information stabilizes estimates in regions with sparse variant counts. The Bayesian framework also yields full posterior distributions for each region, enabling principled quantification of uncertainty in the intolerance estimates.
software.provean: PROVEAN (Protein Variation Effect Analyzer) is a software tool which predicts whether an amino acid substitution or indel has an impact on the biological function of a protein.
software.pyahocorasick: pyahocorasick is a fast and memory efficient library for exact or approximate multi-pattern string search meaning that you can find multiple key strings occurrences at once in some input text.
software.pybedtools: The BEDTools suite of programs is widely used for genomic interval manipulation or "genome algebra". pybedtools wraps and extends BEDTools and offers feature-level manipulations from within Python.
software.pysam: Pysam is a python module for reading and manipulating files in the SAM/BAM format. The SAM/BAM format is a way to store efficiently large numbers of alignments (Li 2009), such as those routinely created by next-generation sequencing methods.
software.pyspade: pySpade performs the whole transcriptome analysis of single cell perturbation datasets.
software.pySpade: Performs whole transcriptome Differential Expression analysis of single cell perturbation datasets
software.qbic-pbm: OLS models to predict non-coding variant effects on TF binding using uPBM
software.qbic-selex: bias-corrected method to predict non-coding variant effects on TF binding using HT-SELEX
software.reddy-starrseq-read-processing: Reddy lab's scripts for processing FASTQs produced from STARR-seq.
software.revel: REVEL is an ensemble method for predicting the pathogenicity of missense variants based on a combination of scores from 13 individual tools: MutPred, FATHMM v2.3, VEST 3.0, PolyPhen-2, SIFT, PROVEAN, MutationAssessor, MutationTaster, LRT, GERP++, SiPhy, phyloP, and phastCons.
software.rna004_130bps_sup@v5.2.0: A basecalling model that can be utilized with the Oxford Nanopore Dorado software.
software.rna004_130bps_sup@v5.2.0_2OmeG@v1: A basecalling model that can be utilized with the Oxford Nanopore Dorado software.
