software.rna004_130bps_sup@v5.2.0_pseU_2OmeU@v1: A basecalling model that can be utilized with the Oxford Nanopore Dorado software.
software.roulette_predict: Predict gemline de novo mutation rates of individual variants using a basepair resolution model for the human genome
software.rsem: RSEM, an user-friendly software package for quantifying gene and isoform abundances from single-end or paired-end RNA-Seq data
software.samtools: Samtools is a suite of programs for interacting with high-throughput sequencing data. SAMtools implements various utilities for post-processing alignments in the SAM format, such as indexing, variant caller and alignment viewer, and thus provides universal tools for processing read alignments (PMID:19505943).
software.sc-linker: sc-linker is for integrating single-cell RNA-seq (scRNA-seq), epigenomic maps and GWAS summary statistics to infer the underlying cell types and processes by which genetic variants influence disease
software.scanpy: Scanpy is a scalable toolkit for analyzing single-cell gene expression data built jointly with anndata.
software.scATAC-processing: Scripts used for processing Shendure lab's scATAC-seq.
software.scdrs: single-cell Disease Relevance Score (scDRS), that links scRNA-seq with polygenic risk of disease at individual cell resolution without the need for annotation of individual cells to cell types; scDRS identifies individual cells that show excess expression levels for genes in a disease-specific gene set constructed from GWAS data.
software.sce2g: Software to make enhancer-gene predictions with  scE2G
software.sceptre: Data analysis R package for single-cell CRISPR data.
software.scipy: SciPy (pronounced "Sigh Pie") is an open-source software for mathematics, science, and engineering. It includes modules for statistics, optimization, integration, linear algebra, Fourier transforms, signal and image processing, ODE solvers, and more.
software.scorpion: an R package that uses coarse-graining of single-cell/nuclei RNA-seq data to reduce sparsity and improve the ability to detect the gene regulatory network's underlying correlation structure
software.seaborn: Seaborn is a Python visualization library based on matplotlib. It provides a high-level interface for drawing attractive statistical graphics.
software.seurat: R toolkit for single cell genomics
software.sharpr: Systematic High-resolution Activation and Repression Profiling with Reporter-tiling (SHARPR). High resolution inference of activing and repressive bases from MPRA tiling designs.
software.shendure-bulk-scqer-scripts: Scripts for generating element quantifications associated with Shendure's bulk scQer data.
software.shendure-sccrispra-custom-seurat-scripts: Scripts for differential analysis associated with Shendure's scCRISPRa data.
software.shendure-scqer-scripts: Scripts for generating element quantifications associated with Shendure's scQer data.
software.sift: SIFT (Sorting Intolerant From Tolerant) is a program that predicts whether an amino acid substitution affects protein function.
software.sift4g: The SIFT algorithm predicts whether an amino acid substitution is deleterious. SIFT 4G (SIFT for genomes) is a faster version of SIFT that enables practical computations on reference genomes.
software.single-cell-uniform-pipeline-subwf-atac-: The ATACseq subworkflow of the single cell uniform pipeline
software.single-cell-uniform-pipeline-subwf-rna-w: The RNAseq subworkflow of the single cell uniform pipeline
software.snakemake: The Snakemake workflow management system is a tool to create reproducible and scalable data analyses. Workflows are described via a human readable, Python based language. They can be seamlessly scaled to server, cluster, grid and cloud environments, without the need to modify the workflow definition. Finally, Snakemake workflows can entail a description of required software, which will be automatically deployed to any execution environment.
software.snapatac2: Singel Cell ATAC-seq (scATAC) analysis and clustering
software.space-ranger: Space Ranger is a set of analysis pipelines that process 10x Genomics Visium data with brightfield or fluorescence microscope images, allowing users to map the whole transcriptome in a variety of tissues.
software.spliceQTL: spliceQTL a method for identifying splice variants that effect RNA splicing
software.splitcode: splitcode is a tool that enables flexible and efficient parsing, interpreting and editing of sequencing reads.
software.star: STAR (Spliced Transcript Alignment to a Reference) aligns short and bulk RNA-seq reads to a reference genome using uncompressed suffix arrays.
software.statsmodels: statsmodels is a Python package that provides a complement to scipy for statistical computations including descriptive statistics and estimation and inference for statistical models.
software.tabix: Tabix is the first generic tool that indexes position sorted files in TAB-delimited formats such as GFF, BED, PSL, SAM and SQL export, and quickly retrieves features overlapping specified regions. Tabix features include few seek function calls per query, data compression with gzip compatibility and direct FTP/HTTP access.
software.tapseq-r-package: Design TAP-seq primers for a set of target genes for targeted scRNA-seq
software.tcsc: Tissue co-regulation score regression(TCSC) jointly analyzes GWAS and gene expression data to disentangle causal tissues from tagging tissues and partition disease heritability (or covariance between two diseases/traits) into tissue-specific components.
software.tgfm: Scripts developed to reproduce analysis described in "Fine-mapping causal tissues and genes at disease-associated loci" (Strober et al. medRxiv 2023).
software.topyfic: Topyfic is a Python library designed to apply rLDA to single_cells/bulk RNA-seq data to recover meaningful topics that involve the key genes like transcription factors involved in different steps.
software.varity: VARITY is an approach for pathogenicity prediction, which has been specifically optimized for rare missense variation.
software.vireo: Variational Inference for Reconstructing Ensemble Origin by expressed SNPs in multiplexed scRNA-seq data.

output_types.aggregated calibrated coding variant eff: A file mapping coding variants to their aggregated experimental and predictive calibrated evidence across functional assays and computational predictors.
output_types.alignments: The mapping locations of input reads with respect to a genome.
output_types.allele specific binding: Data describing differential binding events associated with specific alleles.
output_types.allele specific RNA pseudobulk counts: Allele specific pseudobulk cluster by gene name count matrix.
output_types.allele specific sparse gene count matrix: A matrix where rows represent genes IDs from two individual strain or donor reference genomes (as described in the file format document) and columns represent samples or cells, containing counts of sequencing reads that map to each gene.
output_types.annotated multimodal CRISPR matrix: An object storing single cell CRISPR screen data, integrating multiple modalities such as raw RNA count matrices, sample-level metadata, gene-level metadata, guide-level data, experiment-level metadata, etc.
output_types.annotated sparse gene count matrix: A matrix where rows represent genes and columns represent samples or cells, containing both counts of sequencing reads that map to each gene and cell annotation data.
output_types.annotated sparse peak count matrix: A matrix used in assays like ATAC-seq or ChIP-seq, where rows represent genomic regions (peaks) and columns represent samples or cells, containing both counts of sequencing reads that map to each peak and cell annotation data.
output_types.barcode onlist: A list of all known barcode sequences that have been included in the assay kit and are available during library preparation.
output_types.barcode replacement: A file containing original barcodes and the new barcodes used to replace the original barcodes. One common application is to use in preprocessing Parse SPLiT-seq data with the single cell uniform pipeline.
output_types.barcode to cluster mapping: A barcode to cluster mapping file lists the unique sequencing barcodes used in a single-cell experiment and their corresponding cluster label after using a clustering algorithm.
output_types.barcode to donor mapping: A file describing the mapping of barcodes to the donors of samples.
output_types.barcode to element mapping: A file describing the mapping of barcodes to genomic elements.
output_types.barcode to hashtag mapping: A file describing the mapping of barcodes to hashtags.
output_types.barcode to sample mapping: A file describing the mapping of barcodes to biological sample. Formatting should follow https://github.com/IGVF-DACC/checkfiles/blob/dev/src/schemas/table_schemas/barcode_to_sample_mapping.json.
output_types.barcode to TF overexpression mapping: A file describing the mapping of barcodes to TF overexpression.
output_types.barcode to variant mapping: A file describing the mapping of barcodes to variants.
output_types.barcode to well mapping: A file describing the mapping of barcodes to wells.
output_types.bin paired count: Occurrences of contact between chromatin pairs within designated binned intervals.
output_types.calibrated coding variant effect thresho: A file contains calibration thresholds for coding variant effects defining score ranges for ACMG/AMP evidence levels (PS3/BS3 Supporting through Very Strong) for all functional assays.
output_types.calibrated coding variant effects: A file that maps coding variants to their calibrated ACMG/AMP evidence strengths towards pathogenic or benign evidence.
output_types.caQTL: A file containing results from chromatin accessibility quantitative trait loci (caQTLs) studies.
output_types.cell annotations: A file containing cell classifications and/or labels based on their unique transcriptome and genomic signatures, usually generated from single cell sequencing experiments.
output_types.cell hashing barcodes: The barcodes used for cell hashing.
output_types.chromatin contact quantifications: A file containing chromatin conformation contacts.
output_types.cis-trans regulatory differences: Classification by gene for type of cis- or trans-acting regulation.
output_types.coding variant effects: A file predicting effects of amino acid changes at each position of a given protein.
output_types.coding variants: Variants located within protein-coding regions of genes.
output_types.coexpression: Data describing correlated expression patterns between genes.
output_types.contact matrix: A matrix used in genomic assays, particularly in chromosome conformation capture techniques like Hi-C, to represent the frequency of physical interactions between different regions of the genome.
output_types.control signal: A signal coverage track used as a control, representing read coverage from a baseline or reference sample for comparison purposes.
output_types.covariance matrix: A matrix representing the covariance between multiple variables in a regression model.
output_types.detected tissue: The portion of the tissue section that has been identified and analyzed for gene expression, based on the spatial transcriptomics data captured by the Space Ranger pipeline.
output_types.differential chromatin contact quantific: Details differences in chromatin interactions between experimental conditions.
output_types.differential element quantifications: A file that details the change in effect sizes for a specific non-coding element between two experimental conditions.
output_types.differential gene expression quantificat: The quantified changes in gene expression levels between different conditions or groups.
output_types.differential guide quantifications: The quantified differences in guide RNA (gRNA) abundance or activity between experimental conditions.
output_types.differential peak quantifications: The quantified changes in peaks detected between experimental conditions.
output_types.differential transcript expression quant: The quantified changes in expression levels of transcripts between conditions or groups.
output_types.DNA footprint scores: A file containing DNA footprint scores, which represents the binding sites between DNA and proteins called from assays like ATAC-seq data.
output_types.documentation (readme): Human-readable documentation describing the contents or usage of a dataset.
output_types.domain constraint: A file containing model estimates for the probability that a variant observed in the population is missense within a protein domain or interdomain region.
output_types.drugs: Information describing drugs or chemical compounds.
output_types.DUAL-IPA score: A z-score that quantifies the relative abundance of a protein variant by comparing its GFP expression in HEK293T cells to that of the reference allele, with both signals normalized to mCherry expression as a control.
output_types.edge weights: A representation of the weights assigned to the edges in a graph or network model.
output_types.editing templates: The homology-directed DNA repair (HDR) templates containing SNVs in the library of saturation genome editing assays.
output_types.element quantifications: The quantified levels of specific genomic elements.
output_types.element to gene interactions: A file summarizing the results of an E2G based prediction to identify the linkage between genomic elements and genes.
output_types.elements reference: References for the genomic elements studied, including details like genomic coordinates or identifiers.
output_types.eQTL: A file containing results from expression quantitative trait loci (eQTLs) studies.
output_types.exclusion list: An exclusion list file contains a set of genomic regions or specific sequences that should be ignored or excluded during data analysis.
output_types.exclusion list regions: A file containing a list of genomic coordinates that exhibit anomalous, unstructured, or high signal in most sequencing experiments across diverse cell context or experiments.
output_types.exon constraint: A file containing model estimates for the probability that a variant observed in the population is missense within an exon.
output_types.external source data: File containing a list of urls used as a source.
output_types.feature weights: A representation of the weights assigned to the features in a model.
output_types.fiducial alignment: The alignment of tissue sections using fiducial markers which are reference points embedded in the slide that help to accurately map spatial transcriptomics data to the physical location of the tissue on the slide.
output_types.filtered feature barcode matrix: A matrix output from Cell Ranger, where rows represent features and columns represent barcoded spots or cells. It contains counts of sequencing reads after filtering out low-quality or low-abundance features.
output_types.fold change of post-selection and pre-se: The log2 fold change of the variant allele ratio (variant reads divided by reference reads) between post-selection and pre-selection for each variant.
output_types.fold change over control: The ratio of expression levels or other measurements in an experimental condition relative to a control.
output_types.fragments: The quantified levels of ATAC-seq fragments.
output_types.functional conservation mapping: Mappings describing conserved functional features across species.
output_types.functional impact score: The functional impact scores file reports quantitative metrics indicating how genetic variants affect protein localization (based on GFP fluorescence channel data) and cellular morphology (based on DNA, mitochondria, and actin/Golgi/plasma membrane staining). These scores identify variants causing significant deviations in protein localization or morphological characteristics compared to technical baseline controls.
output_types.gene disease links: Associations between genes and diseases or phenotypes.
output_types.gene program regulators: Predicted regulators of a gene program.
output_types.gene programs: Predicted scores of each gene’s representation in a program (i.e. a latent variable inferred from a factor analysis approach)
output_types.gene universe: List of gene Ensembl ID and symbol using official IGVF GENCODE version (V43), used for downstream analysis of gene networks by the Cellular Program Networks WG.
output_types.genes in pathway: Mappings describing which genes participate in specific biological pathways.
output_types.genome index: A reference file consisting of genome index which allows for faster alignment of sequencing reads to the genome.
output_types.genome reference: A sequence file assembled to represent the reference genome for a specific species.
output_types.global differential expression: A file listing differentially expressed genes across the entire dataset.
output_types.GO annotation: Annotations linking genes or proteins to Gene Ontology terms.
output_types.graph structure: A description of the overall layout and connections within a graph or network.
output_types.guide quantifications: Information and sequencing read counts for each gRNA in a pooled CRISPR screen experiment.
output_types.guide RNA sequences: The sequences of guide RNAs designed for genome editing using CRISPR. Formatting should follow https://github.com/IGVF-DACC/checkfiles/blob/dev/src/schemas/table_schemas/guide_rna_sequences.json.
output_types.guide RNA sequences reference: A reference file comprised of concatenated guide RNA sequences intended for use as an alignment or mapping reference. Unlike 'guide RNA sequences' on tabular file, which follow a standardized tabular format with metadata about each guide, this file contains only the raw sequences.
output_types.GWAS: Results or annotations from genome-wide association studies.
output_types.heritability enrichment: A file with enrichment scores identifying if genomic annotations contribute disproportionately to the heritability of a trait.
output_types.high resolution tissue: A detailed representation of tissue sections at a higher spatial resolution, used for precise mapping and analysis of gene expression patterns within the tissue.
output_types.inclusion list: An inclusion list file contains a set of specific genomic regions or sequences that should be included or focused on during data analysis.
output_types.index: A file that accompanies another file and provides indexed random access to that file.
output_types.index plate: Describes the plate layout of sample index combinations (e.g., i7/i5) used for multiplexed sequencing libraries.
output_types.individual cells profile: CSV file with morphological feature measurements for each imaged cell.
output_types.kallisto single cell RNAseq output: A tar.gz file containing the output of the kb-python count run directory using the nac mode containing everything except for the h5ad file chosen to be the primary result.
output_types.linkage disequilibrium: Information describing non-random associations between genetic variants.
output_types.local differential expression: A file listing differentially expressed genes within a defined genomic region surrounding a perturbation.
output_types.low resolution tissue: A representation of tissue sections at a lower spatial resolution, typically used for initial alignment and quality control purposes in spatial transcriptomics.
output_types.machine learning model features: The features used in a machine learning model.
output_types.marker gene activity: A set of cell-type-specific genes identified in pseudobulked data sets with gene activity scores (GAS). These scores infer potential expression levels from single-cell ATAC-seq data by quantifying chromatin accessibility within and around specific gene loci.
output_types.marker genes: A list of genes that are relevant to the cell type of a pseudobulked data set based on the gene expression levels.
output_types.methylated reads: Basecalled and aligned reads from direct RNA sequencing which includes methylation data.
output_types.methylation count matrix: A h5ad object containing CpG-level methylation counts/coverage per cell.
output_types.methylation counts: A CpG-level summary of methylation counts and coverage.
output_types.minus strand modification state: A file with the bases on the minus strand where a DNA or RNA modification was detected.
output_types.mitochondrial DNA heteroplasmy: A .rds file generated by the mgatk tool, containing mitochondrial genome sequencing metrics derived from single-cell data.
output_types.model parameters: Parameters needed for running computational models to make predictions, or hyperparameters utilized during the training of computational models.
output_types.modification state: An unstranded file with the bases where a DNA or RNA modification was detected.
output_types.motif protein mapping: Mappings describing associations between sequence motifs and proteins.
output_types.motifs: Reference definitions of recurring sequence patterns.
output_types.MPRA sequence designs: Sequences designed for an MPRA experiment. Formatting should follow https://github.com/IGVF-DACC/checkfiles/blob/dev/src/schemas/table_schemas/mpra_sequence_designs.json.
output_types.Nanopore reads: Signal measured from DNA or RNA fragments generated by Nanopore sequencing technology stored in pod5 file format.
output_types.nascent transcriptome index: A reference file consisting of nascent transcriptome index which contains both nascent transcripts and cDNAs and therefore can be used for applications where quantifying intron-spanning reads is important, such as single-nucleus RNA-seq or biophysical models that jointly consider spliced and unspliced transcripts.
output_types.neural network architecture: A representation of the structure of a neural network.
output_types.neural network architecture and edge wei: A representation of the structure and edge weights of a neural network e.g. from TensorFlow.
output_types.normalized variants profile: Multi-dimensional feature profile for each sample.
output_types.ontology terms: Controlled vocabulary terms organized within an ontology.
output_types.orthologs: Mappings describing orthologous genes across species.
output_types.PacBio consensus reads: Consensus sequences of bases derived from PacBio (Pacific Biosciences) sequencing, such as HiFi reads, stored in BAM file format.
output_types.PacBio subreads: Individual sequences of bases corresponding to DNA or RNA fragments generated by PacBio (Pacific Biosciences) sequencing technology stored in BAM file format.
output_types.pathogenicity validation: A file containing statistical estimates of the odds ratio for condition occurrence based on variant classifications
output_types.peak quantifications: The quantified levels of peaks detected in ChIP-seq or ATAC-seq assays.
output_types.peaks: Detected genomic regions of relative enrichment in coverage data, such as peaks in ChIP-seq or ATAC-seq indicating protein binding sites or chromatin accessibility.
output_types.per cell type enrichment scores: Enrichment of eQTL-GWAS co-localization signals in different cell subtypes
output_types.pipeline parameters: The input files used in a computational pipeline.
output_types.plus strand modification state: A file with the bases on the plus strand where a DNA or RNA modification was detected.
output_types.position weight matrix: A matrix comprising the probability of each nucleotide occurring at each position in a sequence motif.
output_types.pQTL: Quantitative trait loci linking genetic variants to protein abundance differences.
output_types.predicted gene regulatory networks: A file containing predicted gene regulatory networks, including interactions between transcription factors and their target genes, and the effect sizes of each component within the networks.
output_types.prime editing guide RNA sequences: The sequences of guide RNAs designed for prime editing CRISPR experiments. Formatting should follow https://github.com/IGVF-DACC/checkfiles/blob/dev/src/schemas/table_schemas/prime_editing_guide_rna_sequences.json.
output_types.primer sequences: The sequences of primers used in an experiment.
output_types.primers table: A tab-separated file containing all TAP-seq primers used to readout the expression of target genes.
output_types.primers track: A bed file used to display genomic positions of TAP-seq primer binding sites in a genome browser.
output_types.probes track: A bed file used to display the genomic positions of hybrid capture probe binding regions in a genome browser.
output_types.protein language model: A model trained on amino acid sequences for representation and prediction of protein properties and function.
output_types.protein protein interactions: Data describing physical or functional interactions between proteins.
output_types.protein sequences: A table listing protein sequences, which may be called from IGVF data or collected from external resources.
output_types.protein stability fluorescence score: GFP and mCherry fluorescence readings in HEK293T cells expressing the reference or variant alleles.
output_types.protein to protein interaction score: Scores or metrics for interactions between proteins.
output_types.protein-protein interaction edge-perturb: Measurement of the impact of a variant on an individual protein-protein interaction, expressed as the difference or fold-change in the protein-protein interaction growth score between the variant allele and the reference allele.
output_types.protein-protein interaction edgotyping s: An aggregated score to measure the overall impact of a variant on all associated protein-protein interactions, relative to the reference.
output_types.pseudobulk annotations: Pseudobulk cluster names for allele specific expression with scores and mouse strain information.
output_types.raw feature barcode matrix: A matrix output from Cell Ranger, where rows represent features and columns represent barcoded spots or cells. It contains raw counts of sequencing reads before any filtering or normalization.
output_types.raw signal: The initial signal coverage track representing the unprocessed read counts across the genome before any normalization or statistical adjustments.
output_types.read count: Number of sequencing reads mapped to reference or variant alleles in different conditions or repeats.
output_types.read-depth signal: A signal coverage track that has been normalized by read depth, providing coverage values adjusted to account for variations in sequencing depth across samples or regions.
output_types.reads: Individual sequences of bases corresponding to DNA or RNA fragments stored in FASTQ file format.
output_types.regional constraint: A file containing a constraint metric defined as the probability that a variant observed in the population is missense, estimated within a genomic region.
output_types.reporter elements: Stores the raw format statistical activity analysis for elements associated with a reporter assay. Formatting should follow https://github.com/IGVF-DACC/checkfiles/blob/dev/src/schemas/table_schemas/reporter_elements.json.
output_types.reporter experiment: Stores the complete raw measurements from a reporter assay. Formatting should follow https://github.com/IGVF-DACC/checkfiles/blob/dev/src/schemas/table_schemas/reporter_experiment.json.
output_types.reporter experiment barcode: Store the raw measurement on a barcode level from a reporter assay.
output_types.reporter genomic element effects: Defines the activity of an element/region within a genome associated with a reporter assay. Formatting should follow https://github.com/IGVF-DACC/checkfiles/blob/dev/src/schemas/as/reporter_genomic_element_effects.as.
output_types.reporter genomic variant effects: Defines the activity of a variant within a genome associated with a reporter assay. Formatting should follow https://github.com/IGVF-DACC/checkfiles/blob/dev/src/schemas/as/reporter_genomic_variant_effects.as.
output_types.reporter variants: Stores the raw format statistical activity analysis for variants associated with a reporter assay. Formatting should follow https://github.com/IGVF-DACC/checkfiles/blob/dev/src/schemas/table_schemas/reporter_variants.json.
output_types.sample barcode count matrix: A matrix of cells (rows) by MULTI-seq sample barcode (columns) count.
output_types.sample sort parameters: The parameters/quantifications used for sorting biological samples, such as those in flow cytometry.
output_types.scale factors: Normalization coefficients applied to raw data to adjust for technical variability and ensure comparability across different samples or regions.
output_types.selected normalized variants profile: Subset of aggregated output (normalized) from pyCytominer, which involves trimming out features that are redundant or non-variable across the dataset.
output_types.seqspec: A machine-readable specification for libraries produced by genomics assays that facilitates standardization of preprocessing and enables tracking and comparison of genomics assays.
output_types.sequence attributes: A dictionary of signals that represents sequence contribution scores across the genome for a given prediction from deep learning models, for example the npz format signals inferred by DeepLIFT from models trained with ATAC-seq data.
output_types.signal: A signal coverage track representing the density or intensity of sequencing reads across the genome.
output_types.signal of all reads: A signal coverage track that includes the combined coverage from all reads, both unique and multimapping, across the genome.
output_types.signal of unique reads: A signal coverage track that represents the coverage specifically from unique reads, excluding multimapping reads.
output_types.signal p-value: Nucleotide resolution signal coverage track, expressed as a p-value to reject the null hypothesis that the signal at that location is present in the control.
output_types.SNP effect matrix: A matrix for estimation of consequence of each single nucleotide mutation in a transcription factor binding site motif.
output_types.sparse gene count matrix: A matrix where rows represent genes and columns represent samples or cells, containing counts of sequencing reads that map to each gene.
output_types.sparse peak count matrix: A matrix used in assays like ATAC-seq or ChIP-seq, where rows represent genomic regions (peaks) and columns represent samples or cells.
output_types.sparse transcript count matrix: A matrix where rows represent transcripts and columns represent samples or cells, containing counts of sequencing reads that map to each transcript.
output_types.spike-ins: Nucleic acid fragments of known sequence and quantity used for calibration in high-throughput sequencing.
output_types.spliceQTL: A file containing results from splicing quantitative trait loci (sQTLs) studies.
output_types.target transcripts: A GTF file containing annotations of targeted transcripts by the TAP-seq primer panel.
output_types.TF binding scores: A signal track represents the transcription factor binding scores across the genome, called from assays such as ATAC-seq data.
output_types.tissue positions: The spatial coordinates or positions of tissue samples or sections in experiments.
output_types.transcriptome alignments: The mapping locations of input reads with respect to a transcriptome.
output_types.transcriptome annotations: Detailed information about the transcripts detected in a genomic assay, including their genomic coordinates, gene names, and other relevant annotations.
output_types.transcriptome index: A reference file consisting of standard transcriptome index which does not contain introns and is therefore only suitable for quantifying bulk and single-cell RNA-seq.
output_types.transcriptome reference: A reference file consisting of all known transcripts or coding regions of the genome.
output_types.UMAP plot: Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection plot SVG two-dimensional plot of the dimensionality reduced feature profile using optimized hyperparameters (Xia et al, Nature Comms, 2024)
output_types.variant binding effects: The genetic variant effects on transcription factor binding affinity.
output_types.variant disease links: Associations between genetic variants and diseases or phenotypes.
output_types.variant drug annotations: Annotations describing relationships between variants and drug response.
output_types.variant effects: The genetic variant effects on gene expression.
output_types.variant functions: Predictions of the functional effects of genetic variants.
output_types.variant localization impacts: Output of a variant painting experiement describing the measured impact of variants.
output_types.variant pathogenicity: A file containing variant pathogenicity scores.
output_types.variant to cluster mapping: A file that maps variants to specific morphological "clusters" or groups of similar phenotypes.
output_types.variant to element mapping: A file describing the mapping of genetic variants to genomic elements.
output_types.variants: A table listing genetic variants, which may be called from IGVF data or collected from external resources.

ESM-1v.combined_score: A combined score that represents the ultimate protein stability prediction for this set of 5 models. Outside of regions of overlap and in the first 20 positions of an overlap, this is the average of the 5 prior segment model scores. In the last 20 position of an overlap, this is the average of the 5 later segment model scores. Between the 20th and 80th position of an overlap, this is a weighted average of the two averages, governed by a cosine sigmoid (see "Combining scores" for details).
ESM-1v.esm1v_t33_650M_UR90S_1: the masked-marginals score for the substitution yielded by the esm1v_t33_650M_UR90S_1 model. If this position is an overlap between two segments of a long sequence, then this is the score for the prior segment (see "Long sequences" subsection below for details).
ESM-1v.esm1v_t33_650M_UR90S_1_next: the masked-marginals score for the substitution yielded by the esm1v_t33_650M_UR90S_1 model for the later segment in an overlap between two segments of a long sequence (see "Long sequences" subsection below for details). This column is only present in files of long sequences (proteins of more than 1022 amino acids). Even when this column is present, values in this column are present only in overlaps.
ESM-1v.HGVS.p: A description of the AA change following the HGVS standard as closely as reasonable, specifically: Values in this column are a string of the form {sequence}:p.{ref}{pos}{alt}, e.g. ENSP00000005226.7:p.Val772Ala where {sequence} is the ENSP ID of the protein (e.g. ENSP00000005226.7). {ref} is the three-letter abbreviation of the AA that appears in the reference sequence at the substitution position (e.g. Val). {pos} is the one-indexed substitution position as an integer (e.g. 772). {alt} is the three-letter abbreviation of the substituting AA (e.g. Ala). Contrary to HGVS recommendation, these variants are not described at the DNA level. These are in-silico predictions of protein stability that depend solely on AA sequences and are agnostic of DNA and other context. The HGVS recommendation for predicted consequences is to use parentheses notation (e.g. ENSP00000005226.7:p.(Val772Ala)), we do not do this because the amino acid substitutions are neither predicted, nor are they consequences of anything, nor are they observed.

MutPred2.Mechanisms: A JSON list of 58 properties. Each property is given by a dictionary of the form {“Property”: “VSL2B_disorder”, “Posterior Probability”: 0.162, “P-value”: 0.345, “Affected Position”: “Y183”, “Type”: “Gain”}. Type could be “Loss”, “Gain” or “Altered”. The Affected Position would be “-” for mechanisms that are regions.
MutPred2.MutPred2 score: Gives the MutPred2 pathogenicity score between 0 and 1 for the variant. A higher value indicates higher pathogenicity.
MutPred2.Substitution: Gives the single amino acid substitution of the protein in the HGVS (Human Genome Variation Society) format.

VAMP-seq.rep<N>_score: the score calculated for replicate <N>. May also be named “rep<N>_tile<M>_score” for datasets that have multiple tiles. Tiles may or may not be overlapping, depending on the experimental design.
VAMP-seq.score: the overall score calculated for this variant as the mean of all replicate measurements
VAMP-seq.standard_error: the standard error of the overall score
VAMP-seq.variant: amino acid variant identifier in HGVS format, relative to the protein. Protein isoform is specified using an Ensembl protein identifier (ENSP)

differential element quantifications.gene_symbol: Gene Symbol according to Ensembl. Useful for downstream analyses
differential element quantifications.guide_id(s): Comma-separated list of guide ids targeting this element (same ids as in guide metadata file)
differential element quantifications.intended_target_name: Name of target element, i.e. {chr}:{start}-{end}. Same as in guide metadata file
differential element quantifications.notes: (optional) Field for optional notes, e.g. reference or source of data
differential element quantifications.power_at_effect_size_XX: (optional) Estimated statistical power to detect a perturbation effect of effect size XX
differential element quantifications.sample_term_id: UBERON, CL for cell type / biosample
differential element quantifications.sample_term_name: Cell type / biosample name. Useful when aggregating results from multiple experiments
differential element quantifications.sceptre_adj_p_value: FDR adjusted p-value calculated by sceptre
differential element quantifications.sceptre_log2_fc: Log2 fold change effect size calculated by sceptre
differential element quantifications.significant: Specifying whether tested E-G pair is significant
differential element quantifications.targeting_chr: Chromosome name of targeted element Gencode/UCSC notation
differential element quantifications.targeting_end: End coordinate of the targeted element (0-based)
differential element quantifications.targeting_start: Start coordinate of the targeted element (0-based)

DUAL-IPA score.avg_gfp: single cells Mean GFP Fluorescence value per well
DUAL-IPA score.avg_mcherry: single cells Mean mCherry Fluorescence value per well
DUAL-IPA score.CCSB_referenece_orf_id: internal ID for the reference ORF.
DUAL-IPA score.dualipa_abun_change: significative change of abundance determined by thresholds derived from the log likelihood ratio method (True/False)
DUAL-IPA score.dualipa_abun_score: log2 Z-score of allele_wt_ratio: (log2(allele_wt_ratio) - log2(wt_mean)) / log2(wt_std)
DUAL-IPA score.hgvs_orf: nucleotide change based on the reference ORF.
DUAL-IPA score.hgvs_protein: amino acid change in HGVS format based on Ensembl protein ID.
DUAL-IPA score.wt_GFP_mCherry_ratio: ratio of avg_gfp/avg_mCherry of related wild-type measurement

SNP effect matrix.Data: Column 1 (string): HGNC gene name of transcription factor. For TF complexes, gene names
SNP effect matrix.Headers: #BASELINE (ﬂoat): log2-transformed SEM score generated from randomly scrambled kmers that

variant binding effects.alt_score: Predicted binding score at alternate allele. Represents the TF’s binding affinity to the reference sequence relative to the average binding affinity of the TF.
variant binding effects.alt_seq_context: Coordinate range on reference genome that contains the highest SEM score with the alternate allele in the scanned sequence. To recreate seq context for indels, start with this coordinate on the ref genome then make the insertion/deletion as defined by the variant identifier.
variant binding effects.pvalue: Part of the standardized Variant-TF prediction file format. SEMpl predictions do not have p-values, thus this column is N/A for all variant predictions.
variant binding effects.ref_score: Predicted binding score at reference allele. Represents the TF’s binding affinity to the reference sequence relative to the average binding affinity of the TF.
variant binding effects.ref_seq_context: Coordinate range on reference genome that contains the highest SEM score with the reference allele in the scanned sequence
variant binding effects.SEMpl.annotation: Annotation label based on ref_score, alt_score, and SEMpl.baseline columns values to characterize the predicted effect of the variant on TF binding. Can be one of 6 labels: • no_binding - No binding observed at either allele o ref_score, alt_score < 2^(baseline) • binding_unchanged - Binding observed, but ref_score and alt_score are equal o 2^(baseline) < ref_score = alt_score • binding_increased - Binding observed at both alleles and alt_score>ref_score o 2^(baseline) < ref_score < alt_score • binding_decreased - Binding observed at both alleles and alt_score<ref_score o 2^(baseline) < alt_score < ref_score • binding_created - Binding observed at alt allele but not at ref allele o ref_score < 2^(baseline) < alt_score • binding_ablated - Binding observed at ref allele but not at alt allele o alt_score < 2^(baseline) < ref_score
variant binding effects.SEMpl.baseline: Log2-transformed value of average TF binding signal at randomly scrambled endogenous kmers, representing a random baseline where binding is not expected to occur. Used as a threshold to determine annotation labels in the SEMpl.annotation column. This value is specific to a SNP Effect Matrix and thus is the same for all variants in a prediction file.
variant binding effects.variant_effect_score: The log2 ratio between alt allele and ref allele binding scores: 𝑙𝑜𝑔2(𝑎𝑙𝑡_𝑠𝑐𝑜𝑟𝑒 / 𝑟𝑒𝑓_𝑠𝑐𝑜𝑟𝑒). Positive values indicate increased preference for the alt allele, negative values indicate increased preference for the ref allele.

BlueSTARR.cCRE chromosome: chromosome of ENCODE cCRE containing the variant
BlueSTARR.cCRE end coordinate: hg38 end coordinate of ENCODE cCRE containing the variant
BlueSTARR.cCRE start coordinate: hg38 start coordinate of ENCODE cCRE containing the variant
BlueSTARR.Log2FC: Predicted log2 fold-change of variant in STARR-seq
BlueSTARR.SPDI: NCBI SPDI variant identifier (from https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7523648/). Alternate allele inserted resulting in predicted fold-change

SGE.alt: the programmed variant allele at the specified position
SGE.amino_acid_change: the amino acid change caused by the introduction of the variant into the reference sequence
SGE.consequence: the most severe of all annotated consequences of the variant as determined by Variant Effect Predictor (VEP), using the MANE select transcript
SGE.D05_R1_lib1: the integer count of the variant at day 5 in replicate 1.
SGE.D05_R1_lib2: In cases where two targets overlap and a given variant is assayed in each, the integer count of the variant at day 5 in replicate 1 of the second target. Empty if the variant is not covered by two targets.
SGE.D05_R2_lib1: the integer count of the variant at day 5 in replicate 2.
SGE.D05_R2_lib2: In cases where two targets overlap and a given variant is assayed in each, the integer count of the variant at day 5 in replicate 2 of the second target. Empty if the variant is not covered by two targets.
SGE.D05_R3_lib1: the integer count of the variant at day 5 in replicate 3. Empty if only two replicates passed QC.
SGE.D05_R3_lib2: In cases where two targets overlap and a given variant is assayed in each, the integer count of the variant at day 5 in replicate 3 of the second target. Empty if the variant is not covered by two targets. Empty if only two replicates passed QC.
SGE.D13_R1_lib1: the integer count of the variant at day 13 in replicate 1.
SGE.D13_R1_lib2: In cases where two targets overlap and a given variant is assayed in each, the integer count of the variant at day 13 in replicate 1 of the second target. Empty if the variant is not covered by two targets.
SGE.D13_R2_lib1: the integer count of the variant at day 13 in replicate 2.
SGE.D13_R2_lib2: In cases where two targets overlap and a given variant is assayed in each, the integer count of the variant at day 13 in replicate 2 of the second target. Empty if the variant is not covered by two targets.
SGE.D13_R3_lib1: the integer count of the variant at day 13 in replicate 3. Empty if only two replicates passed QC.
SGE.D13_R3_lib2: In cases where two targets overlap and a given variant is assayed in each, the integer count of the variant at day 13 in replicate 3 of the second target. Empty if the variant is not covered by two targets. Empty if only two replicates passed QC.
SGE.exon: the exon of the gene in which the variant is located; or, for non-exonic variants, the SGE exonic target whose genomic context includes the non-exonic variant. The format of this column is ${GENE}_X${EXON}, where GENE is the symbolic gene name and EXON is an integer
SGE.functional_consequence: one of “functionally_normal”, “indeterminate”, or “functionally_abnormal”, representing the predicted functional consequence of the introduction of the variant
SGE.functional_consequence_zscore: the Z score, relative to the distribution of expected neutral variant scores, representing the predicted functional consequence of the introduction of the variant. The Z score is compared to threshold values to assign a functional_consequence label
SGE.hgvs_p: The HGVS p. representation of the variant, using Ensembl protein identifiers
SGE.pos: the 1-based position of the variant, in hg38 coordinate space
SGE.ref: the hg38 reference allele at the specified position
SGE.score: the SGE score quantifies the change in variant frequency over time, provided as log2-fold-change per unit time – here, per day.
SGE.snvlib_lib1: the integer count of the variant in the plasmid library prior to transfection and selection, as inferred from amplicon sequencing of the targeted region.
SGE.snvlib_lib2: In cases where two targets overlap and a given variant is assayed in each, the integer count of the variant in the second plasmid library prior to transfection and selection, as inferred from amplicon sequencing of the targeted region. Empty if the variant is not covered by two targets.
SGE.standard_error: the standard error of the score estimate
SGE.target: the name of the SGE library containing the variant. In cases where an entire exon is covered by a single SGE library, target and exon will be the same. In cases where exons are covered by multiple SGE libraries, target will equal to exon plus a single letter (A, B, C…) appended to the name. As an example, SFPQ_X7B represents the second (B) tile of exon 7 of SFPQ. gene window in which the variant  of the gene,
SGE.variant_qc_flag: one of “PASS”, “FAIL”, or “LOWCOV”, indicating whether the variant passes all QC metrics.

Variant-EFFECTS.effect_allele: The effect allele used for defining effect_size (usually same value as alt ).
Variant-EFFECTS.effect_size: Range: [-1, ∞]. Effect size expressed as fractional change in gene expression of effect_allele versus other_allele. e.g., -0.1 means effect_allele leads to a 10% decrease in gene expression, 0 means no change .
Variant-EFFECTS.fdr_method: Method used for multiple testing correction.
Variant-EFFECTS.fdr_nlog10: -log ₁₀ p-value corrected for multiple hypothesis testing, as appropriate for this experiment.
Variant-EFFECTS.gene: Ensembl ID using official IGVF GENCODE version.
Variant-EFFECTS.gene_symbol: Gene symbol from official IGVF GENCODE version.
Variant-EFFECTS.log2fc: Effect size expressed as log ₂ fold-change in gene expression of effect_allele vs other_allele.
Variant-EFFECTS.other_allele: The other allele (usually same value as ref ).
Variant-EFFECTS.pos: 0-based genome coordinate in GRCh38 from SPDI.
Variant-EFFECTS.power: Range: [0,1]. Power to detect an effect of the observed size.
Variant-EFFECTS.transcript: Ensembl ID for transcript, if measurement is transcript-specific.
Variant-EFFECTS.variant: Variant in canonical SPDI format, e.g. , SPDI demo.
Variant-EFFECTS.VariantID_hg19: Original name of variant from Variant-EFFECTS pipeline.

variant functions.BiosampleTerm: Ontology ID associated with the biosample (e.g., UBERON or NCIT ID)
variant functions.BiosampleTermName: Name of the biosample or tissue (e.g., AGNOSTIC, liver)
variant functions.EffectAllele: The allele predicted to have an effect
variant functions.GOOntology: GO term representing a general molecular function or biological process
variant functions.VariantChr: Chromosome where the variant is located (e.g., chr1)
variant functions.VariantEnd: Genomic end position of the variant (same as start for SNVs)
variant functions.VariantStart: Genomic start position of the variant (1-based)
variant functions.{organ}.TLand: 51 columns each representing a predicted functional variant probability score for {organ}

physical_interactions.Affinity Capture-Luminescence: An interaction is inferred when a bait protein, tagged with luciferase, is enzymatically detected in immunoprecipitates of the prey protein as light emission. The prey protein is affinity captured from cell extracts by either polyclonal antibody or epitope tag.
physical_interactions.Affinity Capture-MS: An interaction is inferred when a bait protein is affinity captured from cell extracts by either polyclonal antibody or epitope tag and the associated interaction partner is identified by mass spectrometric methods. Note that this in an in vivo experiment where all relevant proteins are co-expressed in the cell (e.g. PMID: 12150911).
physical_interactions.Affinity Capture-RNA: An interaction is inferred when a bait protein is affinity captured from cell extracts by either polyclonal antibody or epitope tag and the associated RNA species is identified by Northern blot, RT-PCR, affinity labeling, sequencing, or microarray analysis. Note that this is an in vivo experiment where all relevant interactors are co-expressed in the cell (e.g. PMID: 10747033). If the protein-RNA interaction is detected in vitro, use "Protein-RNA" instead.
physical_interactions.Affinity Capture-Western: An interaction is inferred when a bait protein is affinity captured from cell extracts by either polyclonal antibody or epitope tag and the associated interaction partner is identified by Western blot with a specific polyclonal antibody or second epitope tag (e.g. PMID: 11782448, Fig. 2). This category is also used if an interacting protein is visualized directly by dye stain or radioactivity. Note that this is an in vivo experiment where all relevant proteins are co-expressed in the cell. If the proteins are shown to interact outside of a cellular environment (such as lysates exposed to a bait protein for pull down) this should be considered in vitro and Reconstituted Complex should be used. This also differs from any co-purification experiment involving affinity capture in that the co-purification experiment involves at least one extra purification step to get rid of potential contaminating proteins.
physical_interactions.Biochemical Activity: An interaction is inferred from the biochemical effect of one protein upon another in vitro, for example, GTP-GDP exchange activity or phosphorylation of a substrate by a kinase (e.g. PMID: 9452439, Fig. 2). The "bait" protein executes the activity on the substrate "hit" protein. A Modification value is recorded for interactions of this type with the possible values Phosphorylation, Ubiquitination, Sumoylation, Dephosphorylation, Methylation, Prenylation, Acetylation, Deubiquitination, Proteolytic Processing, Glucosylation, Nedd(Rub1)ylation, Deacetylation, No Modification, Demethylation.
physical_interactions.Co-crystal Structure: An interaction is directly demonstrated at the atomic level by X-ray crystallography (e.g. PMID: 12660736). This category should also be used for NMR or Electron Microscopy (EM) structures, and for each of these cases, a note should be added indicating that it's an NMR or EM structure. If there is no obvious bait-hit directionality to the interaction involving 3 or more proteins, then the co-crystallized proteins should be listed as a complex.
physical_interactions.Co-fractionation: An interaction is inferred from the presence of two or more protein subunits in a partially purified protein preparation (e.g. PMID: 11294905, Fig. 9). If co-fractionation is demonstrated between 3 or more proteins, then add them as a complex.
physical_interactions.Co-localization: An interaction is inferred from two proteins that co-localize in the cell by indirect immunofluorescence only when in addition, if one gene is deleted, the other protein becomes mis-localized. This also includes co-dependent association of proteins with promoter DNA in chromatin immunoprecipitation experiments (write "ChIP" in qualification text box), and in situ proximity ligation assays (write "PLA" in qualification text box).
physical_interactions.Co-purification: An interaction is inferred from the identification of two or more protein subunits in a purified protein complex, as obtained by several classical biochemical fractionation steps, or else by affinity purification and one or more additional fractionation steps. Note that a Western or mass-spec may also be used to identify the subunits, but that this differs from "Affinity Capture-Western" or "Affinity Capture-Mass Spec" because it involves at least one extra purification step to get rid of contaminants (e.g. PMID: 19343713). Typically, TAP-tag experiments are considered to be affinity captures and not co-purification experiments. If there is no obvious bait-hit directionality to the interaction, then the co-purifying proteins should be listed as a complex. If only co-fractionation is demonstrated, i.e. if the interaction is inferred from the presence of two or more protein subunits in a partially purified protein preparation (e.g. PMID: 11294905, Fig. 9), then use "Co-fractionation" instead.
physical_interactions.Cross-Linking-MS (XL-MS): An interaction is detected between two proteins using chemically reactive or photo-activatible cross-linking reagents that covalently link amino acids in close proximity, followed by mass spectrometry analysis to identify the linked peptides (reviewed in PMID: 37406423, PMID: 37104977). Experiments may be carried out with live cells or cell lysates in which all proteins are expressed at endogenous levels (e.g. PMID: 34349018, PMID: 35235311) or with recombinant proteins (e.g. PMID: 28537071).
physical_interactions.Far Western: An interaction is inferred when a bait protein is immobilized on a membrane and a prey protein that is incubated with the membrane localizes to the same membrane position as the bait protein. The prey protein could be provided as a purified protein probe (e.g. PMID: 12857883, Fig. 7).
physical_interactions.FRET: An interaction is inferred when close proximity of interaction partners is detected by fluorescence resonance energy transfer between pairs of fluorophore-labeled molecules, such as occurs between CFP (donor) and YFP (acceptor) fusion proteins in vivo (e.g. PMID: 11950888, Fig. 4).
physical_interactions.PCA: An interaction is inferred through the use of a Protein-Fragment Complementation Assay (PCA) in which a bait protein is expressed as a fusion to either an N- or C- terminal peptide fragment of a reporter protein and a prey protein is expressed as a fusion to the complementary C- or N- terminal fragment, respectively, of the same reporter protein. Interaction of bait and prey proteins bring together complementary fragments, which can then fold into an active reporter, e.g. the split-ubiquitin assay (e.g. PMID: 12134063, Figs. 1,2), bimolecular fluorescent complementation (BiFC). More examples of PCAs are discussed in this paper.
physical_interactions.Protein-peptide: An interaction is inferred between a protein and a peptide derived from an interaction partner. A variety of techniques could be employed including phage display experiments (e.g. PMID: 12706896). Depending on the experimental details, either the protein or the peptide could be the "bait".
physical_interactions.Protein-RNA: An interaction is inferred using a variety of techniques between a protein and an RNA in vitro. By way of contrast, note that "Affinity Capture-RNA" involves protein and RNA that are co-expressed in vivo.
physical_interactions.Proximity Label-MS: An interaction is inferred when a bait-enzyme fusion protein selectively modifies a vicinal protein with a diffusible reactive product, followed by affinity capture of the modified protein and identification by mass spectrometric methods, such as the BioID system PMID: 24255178. This system should not be used for in situ proximity ligation assays in which the interaction is measured by fluorescence, eg. PMID: 25168242, which should be captured as co-localization.
physical_interactions.Reconstituted Complex: An interaction is inferred between proteins in vitro. This can include proteins in recombinant form or proteins isolated directly from cells with recombinant or purified bait. For example, GST pull-down assays where a GST-tagged protein is first isolated and then used to fish interactors from cell lysates are considered reconstituted complexes (e.g. PMID: 14657240, Fig. 4A or PMID: 14761940, Fig. 5). This can also include gel-shifts, surface plasmon resonance experiments and bio-layer interferometry (BLI) experiments. The bait-hit directionality may not be clear for 2 interacting proteins. In these cases the directionality is up to the discretion of the curator.
physical_interactions.Surface Display: An interaction is inferred when a protein of interest is fused to a surface protein and assayed for interaction with another protein or ligand. The surface fusion is often expressed in yeast and termed yeast display PMID: 39322662, but can also be expressed in bacteriophage and termed phage display PMID: 10585717, or other microbes and termed bacterial surface display PMID: 21146237 other expression systems. The displayed protein is then used to screen potential binding partners (such as antibodies, peptides, or other proteins). Binding can then be detected by the presence or cellular uptake (PMID: 39322662, Fig. 1C) of a fluorescent reporter.
physical_interactions.Thermal Shift Assay: An interaction is demonstrated between a protein and a ligand by measuring the change in a protein's thermal stability upon ligand binding. This shift can be measured by nano-differential scanning fluorimetry (Nano-DSF) (e.g.PMID: 36650907, Fig. 2 and 3), differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) (e.g. PMID: 32828280, Fig. 1D) or other methods.
physical_interactions.Two-hybrid: An interaction is inferred when a bait protein is expressed as a DNA binding domain (DBD) fusion, a prey protein is expressed as a transcriptional activation domain (TAD) fusion and the interaction is measured by reporter gene activation (e.g. PMID: 9082982, Table 1).

genetic_interactions.Dosage Growth Defect: Overexpression/increased dosage of one gene causes a growth defect in a strain that is mutated/deleted for another gene (e.g. PMID: 15166139).
genetic_interactions.Dosage Lethality: Overexpression/increased dosage of one gene causes lethality in a strain that is mutated/deleted for another gene (e.g. PMID: 10805723, Fig. 3).
genetic_interactions.Dosage Rescue: Overexpression/increased dosage of one gene rescues the lethality or growth defect of a strain mutated/deleted for another gene (e.g. PMID: 12207708, Fig. 3).
genetic_interactions.Negative Genetic: Mutations/deletions in separate genes, each of which alone causes a minimal phenotype, but when combined in the same cell results in a more severe fitness defect or lethality under a given condition (e.g. PMID: 20093466). This term is reserved for high or low throughput studies with scores.
genetic_interactions.Phenotypic Enhancement: Mutation/deletion/overexpression of one gene results in enhancement of any phenotype (other than lethality/growth defect) associated with mutation/deletion/overexpression of another gene, for example response to DNA damage or transcriptional output (e.g. PMID: 11890933, Fig. 2).
genetic_interactions.Phenotypic Suppression: Mutation/deletion/overexpression of one gene results in suppression of any phenotype (other than lethality/growth defect) associated with mutation/deletion/overexpression of another gene (e.g. PMID: 10073572).
genetic_interactions.Positive Genetic: Mutations/deletions in separate genes, each of which alone causes a minimal phenotype, but when combined in the same cell results in a less severe fitness defect than expected under a given condition (e.g. PMID: 20093466). This term is reserved for high or low throughput studies with scores.
genetic_interactions.Synthetic Growth Defect: Mutations/deletions in separate genes, each of which alone causes a minimal phenotype, but when combined in the same cell results in a significant growth defect under a given condition (e.g. PMID: 12871902, Fig. 8).
genetic_interactions.Synthetic Haploinsufficiency: A genetic interaction is inferred when mutations or deletions in separate genes, at least one of which is hemizygous, cause a minimal phenotype alone but result in lethality when combined in the same cell under a given condition (e.g. PMID: 17167106).
genetic_interactions.Synthetic Lethality: Mutations/deletions in separate genes, each of which alone causes a minimal phenotype, but when combined in the same cell results in lethality under a given condition (e.g. PMID: 14690608).
genetic_interactions.Synthetic Rescue: Mutation/deletion of one gene rescues the lethality or growth defect of a strain mutated/deleted for another gene (e.g. PMID: 14734533, Fig. 1).

_meta.file_output_types_note: Values and enum_descriptions come from File.content_type on *File profiles (merged union; aligns with AnalysisStep output_content_types).
_meta.file_content_type_fields_note: Parsed from file format specification Document attachments linked to IGVF catalog v1.1/v1.2 files, grouped by File.content_type. When multiple specifications map to distinct IGVF Catalog method filter values, fields are nested under those method keys.
